PHASE:        Phase 8 Batch 3 — Sub-batch 3.1 (Users data table) BUILT and committed on feat/user-management-table (commit 6b0ba78), main merged in (auth hotfix now present). All 4 validation gates were green pre-merge (typecheck, lint, 226/226 tests, build); re-verifying post-merge.

AUTH_HOTFIX_LANDED_2026-05-19: fix/auth-pin-version squash-merged to main (commit eb0d2f4) and pushed to origin. Branch deleted local + remote. Root cause was next-intl createMiddleware breaking Next.js 15 standalone prerender — see [[feedback_nextintl_middleware_breaks_static_prerender]].

LAST_DONE:    Phase 8 Batch 3 Sub-batch 1 (Event Detail Modal) shipped via squash-merge to main (commit 3a3b7ae, pushed to origin). Three sub-task commits consolidated:
              - 1.1 (9fd10c3): tRPC backend — event.update + event.addAccompanyingRanger + event.removeAccompanyingRanger procedures on apps/web/src/server/trpc/routers/event.ts. All tenant-scoped. event.update uses .strict() schema with NOT_FOUND on count=0. addAccompanyingRanger has XOR refinement (registeredUserId XOR freetextName). removeAccompanyingRanger verifies tenant ownership before delete. Tests +12.
              - 1.2 (dcfe3bd): UI components — apps/web/src/components/events/{event-detail-modal,accompanying-rangers-input,event-timeline}.tsx (+2 test files). Modal uses shadcn Dialog with title/priority/details(offender/vessel/registration/address/action)/notes-textarea/coordinates/rangers/timeline. AccompanyingRangersInput is dual-mode chip UI (registered user search via user.list + freetext add). Events page wires modal via Maximize2 icon button on each KanbanCard (onPointerDown+onClick stopPropagation so dnd-kit drag still works). Tests +11.
              - 1.3 (71d33cc): EarthRanger push-back — apps/web/src/server/lib/earthranger-push.ts (self-contained PATCH helper, NOT imported from packages/jobs to keep web bundle lean). Wired into event.update mutation as best-effort try/catch; never fails local mutation. Maps {title, priority, eventDetails} → ER {title, priority, event_details}. Tests +8 (6 push-helper + 2 router integration covering happy path + 502 graceful degradation).
              Test deltas across the sub-batch: 140 → 171 (+31). All gates: typecheck ✅ lint ✅ test 171/171 ✅ build ✅. Two-stage review (Rule 25) passed both stages. Also shipped earlier same session: SSE-3 (commit 5b3ef4d — closes Phase 8 Batch 2).

NEXT:         (1) Human does visual check at http://localhost:45204/users (logged in via existing session). Eyeball: row width with 4 inline action buttons (Change Role / Reset Password / Activate-or-Deactivate / Add User in toolbar), filter alignment, badge colors, empty state. Click Add User + Change Role to confirm dialogs trigger.
              (2) If visual is fine → squash-merge feat/user-management-table to main with the commit message Opus suggested (see SUB_BATCH_3_1_SHIP_PLAN below), delete branch, checkpoint STATE.md.
              (3) If row width feels cramped → small follow-up to convert row actions to DropdownMenu (deviation #2 from Opus report).
              (4) After Sub-batch 3.1 ships: Batch 3 COMPLETE (Sub-batch 3 was scoped down to just the table since the backend + 3 dialogs already existed). Then Batch 4 candidates: Patrol Area Planning, Monthly Accomplishment View, Audit Log Viewer, Settings page.

PRE_FLIGHT_FINDING_SUB_BATCH_3 (this session 2026-05-18):
  - Backend (user.ts router) was ALREADY fully implemented (181 LOC, 159-LOC test file). Procedures: create, list (with cursor pagination + role/isActive/search filters), getById, updateRole, resetPassword, deactivate, activate. All RBAC-gated. Lists returns lastLoginAt.
  - 3 of 4 UI files were COMPLETE: create-user-dialog, edit-role-dialog, reset-password-dialog all wired to existing trpc.user.* mutations.
  - Only page.tsx was a STUB (8 lines, "data table pending"). Sub-batch 3 was effectively a single-file UI completion task, not a full backend+UI build.
  - Pre-flight survey saved an estimated ~40K tokens of unnecessary backend work.

SUB_BATCH_3_1_SHIP_PLAN (after human visual ✅):
  Branch:  feat/user-management-table (commit 6b0ba78)
  Files:   +role-badge.tsx, +status-badge.tsx, +__tests__/page.test.tsx, ~page.tsx (stub → full table)
  Tests:   +15 new (suite 211 → 226 all passing)
  Gates:   typecheck ✅ lint ✅ test ✅ build ✅
  Documented deviations (Opus report — all matched existing repo conventions):
    1. useQuery + manual cursor instead of useInfiniteQuery — matches events/page.tsx (repo has zero useInfiniteQuery usages)
    2. Inline action buttons instead of DropdownMenu — matches events/page.tsx (Maximize2 inline button pattern); easier to test
    3. Native <select> instead of shadcn Select — for testability + visual consistency
  Squash-merge command:
    git checkout main && git merge --squash feat/user-management-table
    git commit -m "feat(users): user management data table — Phase 8 Batch 3 Sub-batch 3.1"
    git branch -d feat/user-management-table
  After merge: Sub-batch 3 COMPLETE. Batch 3 COMPLETE.

BROWSER_CHECK_BLOCKERS (why agent couldn't run visual check itself):
  1. Playwright chrome install requires sudo (system deps). Skipped — handed to human.
  2. /users redirects to /login (307). CREDENTIALS.md is gitignored + agent-prohibited from reading per H1/H4 security rules. Agent cannot self-authenticate.
  → Visual check delegated to human (faster than working around the auth gate).

LOCKED_DECISIONS_SUB_BATCH_2 (next governance sync should write these to DECISIONS_LOG.md):
  - Reported-event attribution: STRING MATCH Event.reportedByName === KnownRanger.name (case-insensitive). No schema migration.
  - Patrol-led attribution: STRING MATCH PatrolSegment.leaderErId === KnownRanger.erSubjectId. No schema migration.
  - URL param [id] = KnownRanger.id (canonical ranger entity).
  - Sidebar: NO entry for Rangers — deep-link only.
  - Patrol de-dupe: leader + accompanying on same patrol counted ONCE; "patrol-led" wins in activity timeline.
  - Profile deviation: role + assignedArea NOT surfaced (schema lacks fields). Schema migration deferred to focused Phase 7 follow-up if needed.

DEFERRED_FROM_SUB_BATCH_1 (documented in commit 3a3b7ae body — clean paths, no schema changes needed):

SUB_BATCH_3_1_THRASH_NOTE: Sonnet thrash occurred even though scope was assessed as Tier 1 (pure UI, ≤4 files, no backend). The thrash signal in this codebase appears to fire on UI work that needs to read multiple existing pattern files (events/page.tsx + rangers/page.tsx + tests + components/ui/*) — not just tRPC+Prisma+Zod backend work as memory previously characterized. Consider broadening the Opus default to ALL Marine-Guardian dispatched implementation work, not just backend.
